items.title: Chronomancer Low Intensity Power 31k/33.5k
items.build:
3 Button: https://gw2skills.net/editor/?PiiAwylVwWYIMF2IO0KdNbA-DyQYhohe7bHCKwzICJQFKAZen4IhxG4tExyGA-e

5 Button: https://gw2skills.net/editor/?PiiAwylVwWYMMF2IO0KdNbA-DyQYhohe7bHCKwzICJQFKAZen4IhxG4tExyGA-e

Boon recommendation (look at gear): https://gw2skills.net/editor/?PiiAwylVwWYMMF2IO0KdNTA-DSZYBRDsFuA4g8PjIkAVoAU8dijEGbg3SELbAA-e 

For the most part for boons it doesn't matter as long as you have: 50-60% boon duration + crit capped (100% crit chance) if it's easier for you to do it yourself.
items.cleaned_transcript:
For the three-button rundown, Phantasmal Swordsman is above Well of Action in priority. Spam Split Second, your F1, off cooldown. Make sure to finish your autoattack chains, because sword autoattacks are really strong in this build and are the reason you run sword main hand. It is not a big deal if you miss one, but try to finish them. More or less, use sword skill 5, Phantasmal Swordsman, over Well of Action, and use F1 off cooldown.

For the five-button version, it is pretty much the same as the three-button version, except you are adding in a heal skill and Well of Calamity. Your priority order is sword skill 5, optionally heal skill into sword skill 5 again. Your heal skill, Signet of Ether, will reset your phantasm skills, so it will reset skill 5 and let you use it again. Then use both of your wells after that. Your wells have the same cooldown, so in your mind it should always just be one big well action that you are doing. Then spam F1 off cooldown.

Again, try not to cancel your autoattack chains. If you look at the way I played it, I would hold some cooldowns even though they were up just to wait for that final third autoattack to go off. It is the stab: auto, auto, stab. The stab is the big thing you want to watch out for.

For the overall build rundown, skill 2, Blurred Frenzy, makes you evade attacks and become invulnerable temporarily. Technically, if you want to push the build a little further, this is probably your best sixth button to press. Illusionary Leap spawns a clone at the target, and you can use Swap to swap with the clone, so you can look at it as a blink skill.

Skill 4 spawns a clone if you get attacked, so you can optionally use it if you are about to get hit. Maybe it is better than evading. It is also a CC skill. If you need to CC, use skill 4 two times. Press 4 twice, and then you will shoot out Counter Blade, shoot out the bolt, and get a two-second daze.

Skill 5 is your big damage skill. It spawns a phantasm. You take Chronophantasma as your DPS trait, and what that does is make your phantasm do its attack twice. You get two uses out of Phantasmal Swordsman. That is basically what the build relies on: Chronophantasma, to squeeze more out of fewer buttons with sword 5.

Nothing really crazy is going on trait-wise. It is mostly damage, buffing your illusions, and buffing your phantasms. There is Danger Time, which is the reason we run Well of Action. When you inflict slow, you and your illusions have increased outgoing critical strike damage for a duration. That is why Well of Action is really good.

For your F skills, F2 is not that useful. F3 is a CC skill, so you can use it to CC. If you have a bunch of clones or illusions up, use F3 and you get a daze for each clone you have. Lots of clones means lots of CC. Distortion makes you invulnerable for a little bit. It can have niche uses if you are about to die, so keep it in the back of your mind as your panic button.

I do not use Continuum Split in this build because I think overall it is higher intensity. There is a lot going on, it is also a movement-displacing skill, and it has a really long cooldown. It is definitely useful, though. The way Continuum Split works is that anything you cast during it basically does not count, so you can reset a bunch of cooldowns.

For example, if I have one clone up, or two clones because of my signet, I can use Continuum Split and then use Signet of Humility as a CC skill. The CC goes off, Continuum Split ends, and my CC skill is back up. You can get some really big openers with it. With this build, you could consider entering the fight with skill 3, using Continuum Split, doing some things like precasting Phantasmal Swordsman, and getting a little bit more damage out of your opener. You can use Continuum Split that way or for extra CC, but I do not use it here because it is simpler not to. This build is very low-intensity focused, so there is no point in bringing it in. It can also displace you, so if you randomly press it in a bad spot and then split back to a bad spot, it can be a bad time. Be careful with that.

In terms of utilities, feel free to look through all of them. Heal-skill-wise, you probably do not want to change. For your elite, Gravity Well is a pretty solid CC skill, so you can take Gravity Well. It is less bursty than Signet of Humility for applying CC, because Gravity Well applies CC over three seconds, while Signet of Humility applies 350 defiance break right away. But if you need more CC over time, Gravity Well may be useful. Mass Invisibility is extremely niche, so I would probably not worry about it. Time Warp can be useful if your group is seriously struggling with quickness, or if you decide to play this build as a quickness build, but I would probably stick to Signet of Humility or Gravity Well.

Mesmer has a lot of useful utilities. If you are playing the well build, just leave those skills as they are. If you are playing the other build or the three-button build, you should have a condition signet you can swap out. Well of Precognition gives stability and aegis to you and your group, so it is very good. Well of Senility is extra CC if you need extra CC. Signet of Domination also stuns if you press it, so it can be useful if you are playing the three-button build and want that big stun.

Feedback is a projectile reflect. It is good if you are looking for reflects, for example if you are playing the build on Matthias and need those reflects. There are also boon-strip skills like Null Field and Phantasmal Disenchanter. I would probably run Phantasmal Disenchanter if I needed boon strip.

Portal Entre, or portal in general, is the famous Mesmer portal. You put down a portal in one place and the other portal somewhere else, and you can teleport between them. There are lots of useful cases for that. Blink lets you blink anywhere. It is very useful, for example on Sabetha if you want to dodge the flame wall or just need to reposition. Mesmer has a lot of really good utility, so feel free to look through those skills.

If you are interested in playing this build as quickness or alacrity, look at the last trait line in Chronomancer. You will see Stretch Time and Seize the Moment. By default, you are on Chronophantasma. Taking one of those traits makes it so every time you spawn a phantasm or shatter, you grant alacrity or quickness. If you run Stretch Time, you are an alacrity DPS. If you run Seize the Moment, you are a quickness DPS.

Unfortunately, with the way this build works, you do not really get to spam enough clones or phantasms while just camping sword/sword and spamming your stuff. You are going to need boon duration, and a lot of it. I will probably leave a link in the description if you want to copy me. My recommendation is at least 50%. Try to stick to probably 60% to 70% if you want to be 100% safe and have a good amount of overflow on quickness or alacrity.

I am running Diviner's for all my back pieces and accessories: power, precision, ferocity, and concentration. Concentration is the boon-duration stat. With this, you trade off power, precision, and ferocity. To make up for the lost precision, I switched to full assassin's armor so I have enough precision to stay crit capped. Then I run berserker's swords with a sigil of accuracy. To maintain quickness or alacrity, you need a solid amount of boon duration.

You probably will not be able to play the three-button build as a boon giver, because the three-button build does not use Signet of Ether. If you decide to play this as the three-button version, swap Well of Action to Signet of Ether. You need to spam those phantasms and get those clones out. Usually with the build you can just spam F1 right away, but for the boon version I would recommend waiting with your first F1 until you have at least two clones. That way, when you shatter, you give a little bit of quickness.

I will probably do the showcase with the five-button version because it already uses Signet of Ether. If you want to play it as three-button, you can completely drop Well of Action and use Signet of Ether to get more Phantasmal Swordsmen out. One thing I will mention is that I will be doing the showcase with Seize the Moment. I decided to do the five-button version, so I apologize for saying otherwise earlier. I will use Seize the Moment because Flow of Time gives you self alacrity, and I want the quickness uptime you see on me to be the quickness uptime you would see on your allies. You can also throw in Illusionary Leap, skill 3, to spawn more clones and get more illusions up for your shatter, but that would be an extra button press.

If you are playing specifically quickness with Seize the Moment, you can also run Time Warp. You would lose out on CC, but it is optional if you want stronger quickness uptime overall. You throw down the big dome, and while your allies are in it, they gain quickness. It can help if you decide to play the quickness variant and want to run a little bit less boon duration. Overall, I hope you enjoy the build, I hope it works out for you, and thank you for watching.

items.title: Chronomancer Low Intensity Condi 32k/34k
items.cleaned_transcript:
Hello. Please use the chapters below to navigate this guide. Thank you.

For the three-button explanation, all you want to do is spam Phantasmal Warlock, use your heal skill to reset Phantasmal Warlock, and use your F2 at three clones. Using F2 at three clones pretty much only applies the very first time you use F2. After that, you will almost always have three clones. But if you end up phasing or something like that, try to wait until you have three illusions or clones up before using F2.

This build relies on spamming your phantasm, because this summons two illusions and then doubles up with Chronophantasma. Basically, you will have a whole bunch of illusions doing a whole bunch of damage. The reason you want to wait until you have three illusions for F2 is because of Illusionary Reversion, which spawns an extra clone if you shatter at three clones.

For the five-button explanation, it is very similar to the three-button version. You want to spam Phantasmal Warlock, your heal skill into Warlock, and then use F2, or Rewinder, at three illusions. The two additions are Chaos Armor and Chaos Storm.

Chaos Armor has no cast time, so you can use it in the middle of any other skill or animation. It will not interrupt anything. It will blind and confuse nearby foes, and it will also give you a chaos aura. Blinding foes will also give them confusion. If you look at Ineptitude, blinding a foe inflicts confusion. So it is a very nice skill to cast, and it also gives you chaos aura. If you look at Illusionary Membrane, chaos aura grants you increased condition damage for a period of time, so you get a decent DPS boost even though the skill itself does not really do all that much.

Skill five is your bottom-priority skill. It is just damage, a little bit more conditions, and a small CC.

For the overall build explanation, you do not use skill two in this build, but you can use it if you want to reposition yourself. You reposition yourself backward, away from whatever your target is. For example, if I use skill two while my back is toward the golem spawner, I will go back toward the golem spawner. It can be a little dangerous if you use it in a bad spot, so I recommend just not using it, but you can use it if the time calls for it.

Skill three is the meat of the build. This is how you do a lot of damage by pressing a few buttons. It summons two phantasms, and they do a whole bunch of damage and inflict torment and bleeding through your traits. You also summon two more because of Chronophantasma. This is the bread and butter of the build. Phantasmal Warlock summons two, they do their thing, and then Chronophantasma summons two more. This is how you generate a lot of clones and a lot of damage, so skill three is your top-priority skill to cast. The same goes for your heal into skill three. You want to pump them out.

Skill four gives a little bit of confusion and blindness next to your opponents, plus chaos aura. Due to your traits, you apply extra confusion through Ineptitude. Chaos aura also works with Illusionary Membrane, so you get a bit of a condition damage boost. Any time you gain chaos aura, you get that boost and do a little bit more damage. The same applies to your F2 shatter. If you use shatter skill two, you get chaos aura.

Skill five is the lowest-priority skill. It is just a little bit of extra damage and extra conditions, so use it whenever it is up.

For your shatters, you can pretty much skip F1. You never want to cast it; it is worse to cast it because you want to leave your illusions up. F2 is the good one to cast, and it is pretty much the only shatter you want to cast. F3 and F4 are conditional. F3 is Time Sink, a daze, so it is CC. For example, if you are doing something like Twin Largos in wing six and you need to CC, use Time Sink. Any time there is a break bar, use Time Sink over F2.

F4 is Continuum Split, a distortion skill, so it is basically invulnerability. If you are in an oh-shit position and you are dying, you can use Continuum Split. I do not use it in this build. I talked about it a little bit in the Power Chrono video, but you can use it as an opener if you want. For example, if the fight is starting and you want to squeeze out a little more damage, you can do something like two into Continuum Split into three, heal, three. Then your three, heal, three is up again, so you can recast three, heal, three. That would do more damage than what I showed, but it is an extra button press and may be a little difficult to navigate.

Part of these builds is that I try my best to avoid forced movement. If you use Continuum Split while you are running away from death, then keep running, you can get snapped back into death. Be careful with Continuum Split, but you can use it as you wish.

You can also combo it with your elite. Your elite does a whole bunch of CC, so you can really carry CC. For example, if you are fighting and there is a break bar, and you are at three clones because the more clones you have with Continuum Split, the longer time you have, you can use Continuum Split, use your big CC skill, use your big CC skill again, go back, and reuse both big CC skills. You can carry break bar damage with Mesmer. It is a lot of break bar damage in very few skills. Consider using that for break bars over damage. That will probably carry the group harder than you think.

For the traits, in Dueling, Sharper Images is part of the reason why you do a lot of damage with not a whole lot. Your clones and phantasms both apply bleeding. This is part of the reason you are able to camp illusions, summon a whole bunch of phantasms, and do damage, because your illusions apply bleeding. It is a whole bunch of bleed spam through Sharper Images.

Some of this is about buffing up F2. F2 applies confusion and blindness, the same as skill four, so it applies a lot of confusion. For example, when the boss is moving around and attacking players, you do more damage.

I will also mention that the build is really torment heavy. You apply a lot of torment through your skill three and skill one. That is a bit of a downside, because torment does more damage to foes that are stationary. On some fights the build may be worse, but on some fights it may be better. For example, if you are spamming torment and the target is not moving, like on Cairn, and it is also using a lot of abilities, you will be able to stack up confusion and do a little more damage than you normally would. It just depends. That is a downside of the build, but I think it is still good to run, and you do not really have to worry about it too much.

Method of Madness gives you Lesser Chaos Storm when you cast your heal skill. I already talked about Illusionary Membrane. Bountiful Disillusionment gives you stability and grants additional boons to nearby allies when you shatter. The additional boons to nearby allies do not matter as much because you are not playing this as a support or healer. The main focus is that you can pump out a little more stability for yourself.

For the Chronomancer line, Time Catches Up is more damage. Flow of Time gives you self-alacrity. The big one is Illusionary Reversion. If you watched the previous video, this was on Danger Time. That was a mistake. I did not realize it at the time, and I guess I did not read the trait properly, but Illusionary Reversion is better because you create an extra clone if you shatter at three clones. If you were running the condi build before, or this specific condi build, swap Danger Time to Illusionary Reversion. It is just better. I think when I initially read it, I missed the generate-an-extra-clone part and thought it only granted extra alacrity or something, so it did not seem that good. Illusionary Reversion is better. This is part of the reason the build is still doing very similar damage even though there has been a nerf, because the build has been slightly improved. Please swap to Illusionary Reversion if you are running the previous build.

Chronophantasma is the bread and butter of how you are able to summon a whole bunch of phantasms.

For your utilities, you never want to touch the heal skill. This is your bread-and-butter skill. It is how you deal a whole bunch of damage with Phantasmal Warlock. Signet of Midnight and Signet of Domination can be left alone; you never want to touch these either. Signet of Domination is a CC skill. Obviously, you trade a little bit of damage because you lose the condition damage, but it is a decent skill: 300 break damage and three seconds of stun. If your group is struggling with CC, please use it.

Signet of Humility can also pretty much stay as is. You have the option of running Gravity Well if you want. Gravity Well is a little more CC, but it is over more time, so it is less bursty. It does have a little more CC if your group is dealing with really large break bars where timing does not matter as much. You can swap to Gravity Well and pump out a little more CC than normal compared to Signet of Humility. Otherwise, I would probably stay on Signet of Humility because burst CC is that good. Again, you can combo Signet of Humility with Continuum Split to get a whole bunch of break damage out, so this is really good.

If you are swapping anything out, you want to swap out Signet of Illusions. Mesmer has a whole bunch of really useful utility skills. Feel free to read through all of these, but some good ones are Well of Precognition, which gives aegis and stability; Well of Senility, which is extra CC; and Feedback, which reflects projectiles. For example, if you are playing this build on Matthias in wing two and your group is looking for Feedback, this is the skill you want to run. This is the reflect skill for dealing with that mechanic.

Portal Entre is the classic Mesmer portal. You put down a portal in one place, put down a portal in another, and teleport your teammates around. It is very useful, for example, on wing three escort or some of the later fights. You probably should not run this unless the encounter calls for it, but Portal Entre is very useful.

Phantasmal Disenchanter is a good boon-rip skill. For example, if you are playing this in fractals and dealing with No Pain, No Gain, you can swap in Phantasmal Disenchanter instead of the clone skill and strip boons from opponents.

Blink lets you teleport to a target location. This is really useful. For example, if you are playing on Sabetha in wing one and dealing with flame walls, you can blink over the flame. You can basically completely nullify that mechanic with one skill change, and hopefully never die to the flame wall unless you mess up your cursor.

Mesmer has so much useful utility. Feel free to look through all of it and read through it. I just showed the strongest or most useful ones, but there is a lot of good stuff here. Run whatever you need as the group calls for it.

If you are interested in playing this build as quickness or alacrity, the only thing you need to change is the last trait in Chronomancer. Seize the Moment gives you and your allies quickness per clone you shatter, and whenever you summon a phantasm. Stretched Time does the same thing but with alacrity. Every time you shatter, based on how many clones you shatter, you grant boons. Any time you summon a phantasm, you grant boons.

Unlike the power version, this build has strong clone generation and strong phantasm generation because the phantasm cooldown is really low. Your F2 cooldown is fairly low, and you spam a lot of illusions, so every time you shatter you should be at three clones. You also gain some natural boon duration from your traits. Chaotic Persistence gives you concentration and expertise while affected by regeneration. If you are playing this build with a healer, you get free concentration. The TL;DR is that the only thing you need to change is to swap one of these traits, and then you are good to go on boons.

The only thing I should mention is that if you are playing quickness and you are really worried about your boon uptime, you have the option of swapping your elite to Time Warp. Time Warp grants you and your allies quickness. It is not really necessary. I would recommend sticking to Signet of Humility any time there is a break bar, but if you are playing this on a fight without a break bar, or for whatever reason your group has no problems with CC and you feel like Signet of Humility is a useless skill that you never use, you can swap to Time Warp and grant a little more quickness.

For the next part, I will show a small demonstration to show that yes, you can keep up 100% quickness with this tiny bit of boon duration. I will do the quickness variant, because if you look at your traits, you gain a lot of self-alacrity generation. I will run Seize the Moment and look at my own quickness uptime to show that you will be fine with this build if you just run Seize the Moment with no boon duration.

You can see that even though I did not have regeneration, so I did not get that extra boon duration and extra concentration, I still had more than enough boon uptime and quickness uptime for myself and therefore for my allies. That is all I had for the build. I hope you all enjoyed it, and I hope you see success with the build. Thank you.
